failedAssertions.reason: Pagination controls missing from DataTable component. With 148 rows (destrieux symbol), table silently truncates to 50 rows. Row count label correctly shows '148 rows' but no Next/Previous/page controls are rendered anywhere in the DOM. The pagination UI is not implemented.

rootCauses.description: DataTable component in the React frontend does not render pagination controls. When row count exceeds 50 (the page size), only the first 50 rows are rendered and no Next/Previous/page number buttons appear. The row count label correctly reports the total, suggesting the pagination state exists but the UI controls are not rendered.
rootCauses.suggestedFix: Add pagination controls to the DataTable component UI: render Next/Previous buttons and current page indicator when totalRows > pageSize. Wire up to existing pagination state (currentPage, totalPages) if it exists, otherwise add pagination state.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated Known UI behaviors for execution milestone: documented serializeVBR fix, new pagination missing bug (VAL-EXEC-008), noted ~exists() parse failure in Sulcal identification example, and symbol selector JS workaround.
